topic: A person discovers the tree they planted as a child has been cut down
raw_output:
[story 1]

The sight hit him like a physical blow. Where the towering oak, his childhood monument, had stood just last autumn, there was now only a jagged, splintered stump and a scattering of pale, stripped wood. His jaw locked so tight he thought his teeth might crack. He stumbled forward, his boots crunching on the dry earth, his hands balling into fists so tight the knuckles shone white. "No," he whispered, the sound raw and vibrating in his chest. He circled the devastation slowly, his gaze sweeping over the broken remnants, a low, guttural sound building in his throat. He kicked a loose piece of bark, the impact sending a jolt of furious energy up his leg. Every instinct screamed for retribution, for someone to answer for this deliberate erasure.

[story 2]

"This is a joke," she spat, her voice dangerously low, the sound scraping against the silence of the clearing. She stared at the exposed roots, the way the soil had been violently torn away. Her breath hitched, coming in short, sharp gasps that hitched with rising intensity. She ran a hand through her hair, pulling at the strands as if trying to physically pull the shock out of her system. Her eyes, usually warm, were narrowed slits of pure, unadulterated fury. She took a step back, planting her feet wide, her body rigid with suppressed violence. "Who did this?" she demanded, the question laced with a venom that promised retribution. She didn't wait for an answer; she just stood there, radiating a coiled, explosive tension.

[story 3]

He knelt down, not to examine the damage, but to touch the raw, exposed earth where the roots used to anchor the giant. A tremor ran through him, starting deep in his core and spreading outward. His vision tunneled; the edges of the world seemed to sharpen into a single, burning point of grievance. He pushed himself up abruptly, his movements jerky and uncontrolled. He looked toward the distant edge of the property, his face contorted, a deep, terrible set to his features. A harsh, ragged exhale escaped him, sounding more like a snarl than a breath. He clenched his fists so hard he felt the tendons strain, his whole frame vibrating with a desperate need to shatter something. He started walking again, a fast, relentless pace that devoured the space around him.
